drupalNodeEvent.title: Taiwan Legal: What Does ROC Law Say about Taiwan?

field_organizers.processed: U.S.-Asia Law Institute at NYU School of Law

drupalNodeEvent.field_event_venue: Furman Hall 326, at 245 Sullivan Street, NY, NY, 10012 and on Zoom

field_description.processed: Taiwan’s status as a state is often challenged not because it fails to meet the criteria for statehood, but because of its ambiguous legal relationship with the People’s Republic of China (PRC). We continue our “Taiwan Legal” speaker series by asking how Taiwan, functioning as the Republic of China (ROC), defines its relationship with the PRC in legal terms. Yu-Jie Chen, an assistant research professor at the Institutum Iurisprudentiae of Academia Sinica, will explain what the ROC Constitution says and how Taiwan engages with and distinguishes itself from the PRC.
